paper_threads.summary: Introduces Hyperagents, an AI system that improves at solving tasks and also improves how it improves itself, building on the Darwin Gödel Machine with generalizable self-improvement capabilities.
The AI Scientist paper on fully automated AI research has been published in Nature, showing scaling laws for AI science and autonomous research capabilities.
Large-scale RL in simulation without demos or reward engineering can solve dynamic, dexterous contact-rich tasks with reactive and forceful learned behaviors.
Multi-Answer RL trains language models to output multiple plausible answers rather than collapsing to a single mode, improving diversity and coverage.
Cursor's Composer 2 tech report shows continual learning with new checkpoints every 5 hours and interesting RL performance scaling studies.
NVIDIA releases new paper on PivotRL for high accuracy agentic post-training at low compute cost, used in Nemotron-3 training.
MolmoWeb release: fully open multimodal web agents built from scratch with Molmo2, including models, datasets, and training code.
Scaling laws paper reveals bias in parabolic IsoFLOP fits used by major labs, potentially misallocating millions in compute resources.

announcements.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live launches as highest quality audio & voice model with lower latency and more natural interactions for voice-first agents.
OpenAI launches Safety Bug Bounty program focused on identifying AI abuse and safety risks across OpenAI products.
Cohere releases transcribe model achieving #1 accuracy on Open ASR Leaderboard for speech-to-text, now available as open source.
Google DeepMind announces partnership with Agile Robots to deploy AI models for complex industrial challenges through their robotics platform.
New Lyria 3 Pro music generation model available to Google AI subscribers in Gemini App, demonstrated with flow state background music.

discussions.summary: McMansion Hell author Kate Wagner criticizes NY Times for repeatedly plagiarizing her architectural criticism work without attribution.
Discussion on how RL has become the dominant AI paradigm while old-school RL researchers remain largely uninterested in the LLM stage.
Conversation about LLMs being good for learning but paradoxically making people feel there's no point since AI already does better.
Criticism of ARC-AGI-3 benchmark claims, pointing out that NetHack has been an unsaturated agentic intelligence benchmark since 2020.
Discussion on how personalization memory in LLMs can be distracting for models, affecting their performance on simple tasks.
